{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|38|29|N|140|59|29|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minamisōma, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = <!-- actually displays as "Periods" --> | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[Haranomachi Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Joban Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|38|29|N|140|59|29|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minamisōma, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = <!-- actually displays as "Periods" --> | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[Haranomachi Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Joban Line]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|38|29|N|140|59|29|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minamisōma, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = <!-- actually displays as "Periods" --> | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of northern [[Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[Haranomachi Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai Kofun]][[en:Sakurai Kofun]][[ja:桜井古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537784]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.641389|N|140.991389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Minamisoma|WD=Q642094}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai Kofun]][[en:Sakurai Kofun]][[ja:桜井古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537784]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.641389|N|140.991389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Minamisoma|WD=Q642094}} | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]], of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from [[:en:Meiji University|Meiji University]] in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen [[:en:Step pyramid|Step pyramid]] 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Haranomachi Station|Haranomachi Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University}} in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen {{ill|Step pyramid|en|Step pyramid|WD=Q1456099|lt=Step pyramid}} 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}}, of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University}} in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen {{ill|Step pyramid|en|Step pyramid|WD=Q1456099|lt=Step pyramid}} 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Haranomachi Station|en|Haranomachi Station|WD=Q5654173|lt=Haranomachi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.

The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University}} in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.641389|N|140.991389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Minamisoma|WD=Q642094}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}}, of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University}} in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen {{ill|Step pyramid|en|Step pyramid|WD=Q1456099|lt=Step pyramid}} 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Haranomachi Station|en|Haranomachi Station|WD=Q5654173|lt=Haranomachi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/tourist/tourist_attractions/historic/culturalproperty/6855.html Minamisoma home page] {{in lang|ja}}

From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Haranomachi Station|en|Haranomachi Station|WD=Q5654173|lt=Haranomachi Station|1=Haranomachi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Sakurai Kofun | native_name =桜井古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.641389|N|140.991389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Minamisoma|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 22,179 sq m | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1955, 1983 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Archaeological Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Kofun '''|桜井古墳|Sakurai kofun}} is the largest of a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1956.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137997|title=桜井古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> with the extent of the designation expanded in 1988.
==Overview== The tumulus is a "two conjoined rectangles" type ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) located on a low plateau approximately 10 meters in elevation above the southern bank of the Nitagawa River. It is part of a cluster of 37 tumuli, both large and small, spread over a 900 meter section of the same {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}}, of which twelve survive. The ''kofun'' drew much attention when first excavated by a team from {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|WD=Q46492|lt=Meiji University|1=Meiji University}} in 1955 as it was the largest ''kofun'' then known in the Tōkohu region. Subsequently, larger ''kofun'' have been found, but the Sakurai Kofun remains the third largest of its type in the region, with a length of 74.5 meters and height of 6.8 meters. . The head of the tumulus is in the form of an earthen {{ill|Step pyramid|en|Step pyramid|WD=Q1456099|lt=Step pyramid|1=Step pyramid}} 45 meters on each side, with a height of 6.35 meters. From the design and artifacts recovered, it is estimated to have been built in the middle of the 4th century.
During excavations by the Haramachi Board of Education in 1983, traces of a surrounding moat with width of 7 to 20 meters wide and depth of 60 to 70 cm. The area including the moat was added to the designated Historic Site designation in June 1988. It was also found that the southern end of the rear portion of the mound had been truncated in antiquity, so that its original length was once longer than 74.5 meters. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' were discovered; however, fragments of two bamboo-shaped wooden sarcophagus were found at the top of the rear portion of the tumulus.
At present, the ''kofun'' is maintained as part of Sakurai Kofun Park, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Haranomachi Station|en|Haranomachi Station|WD=Q5654173|lt=Haranomachi Station|1=Haranomachi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Joban Line|Joban Line]].
